microsoft_ft: **Owned the full deployment lifecycle** of Azure's recovery validation program, scaling from a 2-person operation to a platformized system sustaining 45+ annual resilience drills and driving **$14M+ impact** across enterprise customers including Databricks, Walmart, SAP, and NetApp.

Led **0→1 development** of an internal Resilience Automation Platform, defining product requirements and self-service scheduling capabilities that cut operational toil by 30% and transitioned drill execution to a scalable, repeatable model.

Built and deployed an **internal AI agent** for drill planning, restructuring workflows to reduce planning cycle time by 39% and increase drill capacity by 21% — directly mirroring the agent-build-and-optimize loop central to forward-deployed PM work.

Directed 14 high-visibility cross-org recovery executions, including serving as bridge lead for a sovereign-cloud network isolation test tied to a **$1.5B+ enterprise contract**, managing senior stakeholder relationships throughout.

Pioneered Azure's first proactive rack-level resilience testing capability, delivering the program in 4 months with a **94% recovery rate** and surfacing critical hardware defects that shaped future platform investment.
microsoft_2023: **Drove AI agent adoption** across 14 Azure service teams by leading hands-on demos and training sessions for an AI-powered code generation workflow, saving **37 engineering hours monthly** and closing the gap between deployment and utilization.

Shaped the product roadmap by facilitating **11+ customer discovery sessions** with Azure service teams to surface critical feature gaps, directly influencing the addition of intent-based YAML generation to the platform backlog.

Improved retrieval quality and data integrity by migrating documentation to an **AI-powered semantic search** tool with rigorous metadata standards, cutting lookup time by 83% and demonstrating hands-on LLM tooling experience.
microsoft_2022: **Accelerated region launches by 28%** and generated $3M in revenue by securing cross-functional alignment on a unified automation prioritization framework across 140+ teams — demonstrating the stakeholder leadership and deployment ownership this role demands.

Ran discovery with 20+ service teams to surface **81 hours of manual toil per region**, then built a roadmap to automate critical paths, translating ambiguous operational pain into a structured, actionable deployment playbook.

Built a **Power BI dashboard** tracking automation gaps across 140+ teams, enabling leadership to prioritize high-impact investments and providing the data layer that grounded all roadmap and resourcing decisions.
amazon_robotics: Defined the **migration strategy and dependency map** for a 2,000+ unit legacy OS transition across 1,200+ stations, achieving zero operational downtime and delivering the deployment on schedule under tight cross-team coordination.

Drove strategic alignment across IT, Operations, and Engineering to implement **automated CI/CD pipelines**, accelerating the software deployment cycle by 25% and reducing integration friction for ongoing agent rollouts.

Facilitated Agile ceremonies including sprint planning and retrospectives, **resolving 40+ high-priority tickets** to stabilize the developer intake process and keep the deployment lifecycle on track.
pro_painters: **Increased job bookings by 26%** by redesigning the end-to-end scoping and invoicing lifecycle for 200+ monthly proposals through a new CRM process, demonstrating the operational ownership and customer-facing execution this role requires.

Reduced Customer Acquisition Cost by **13%** and lifted conversions by 2.7% by executing a digital-first go-to-market strategy, optimizing the website and Google profile to improve top-of-funnel performance.
Identified and closed margin leaks by conducting **profitability analysis across 1,000+ project records**, surfacing pricing model improvements that directly improved unit economics for leadership.

tailoring_notes: JD term 'deployment lifecycle' woven into microsoft_ft bullet 1 and microsoft_2022 bullet 1 — mirrors JD phrase 'own the deployment lifecycle from pre-sale scoping to post-go-live optimization'.

JD term 'AI agent' / 'build and optimize AI agents' used substantively in microsoft_ft bullet 3, which describes building an actual internal AI agent and directly calls out the connection to forward-deployed PM work.

JD term 'playbook' referenced in microsoft_2022 bullet 2 ('structured, actionable deployment playbook') mirroring JD responsibility 'improve our playbooks for agent development and customer deployment'.
JD phrase 'customer discovery / workshops / design sessions' reflected in microsoft_2023 bullet 2 ('11+ customer discovery sessions') and microsoft_2022 bullet 2 ('discovery with 20+ service teams').

JD emphasis on 'senior customer stakeholders' and 'owning customer relationships' surfaced in microsoft_ft bullet 4 with explicit senior stakeholder language tied to a named enterprise contract outcome.
